notable.notable_blurb: Only Founding Father to sign all four great

career.notes: Signed the Continental Association (1774), Declaration of Independence (1776), and Articles of Confederation (1777).
Co-author with Oliver Ellsworth of the Connecticut Compromise that resolved representation between large and small states.
Served as mayor until his death.
From Connecticut; served until his death.

cross_connections.description: Great-grandfather of Jane Wakeman Skinner Dwight (wife of Yale's 12th president Timothy Dwight V); his Sherman blood entered the Pope Dwight line through her 1866 marriage.

cross_connections.display_label: Great-nephew through niece Elisabeth Daggett; Civil War commander

narrative_blocks.header: Signed all four founding state papers
narrative_blocks.body: Roger Sherman is the only Founding Father to sign the Continental Association (1774), Declaration of Independence (1776), Articles of Confederation (1777), and United States Constitution (1787) — the four documents that founded the American nation. He co-authored the Connecticut Compromise with Oliver Ellsworth, which gave the new federal legislature its two-chamber structure.

narrative_blocks.header: Self-taught from cobbler to Founding Father
narrative_blocks.body: Roger Sherman began as a cobbler in his father's Stoughton shop and educated himself through borrowed books while working at the bench. He rose to become Mayor of New Haven, US Representative, US Senator, and one of the most influential framers of the federal government — a remarkable arc from manual labor to founding statesman.

narrative_blocks.header: Buried at Grove Street, with Thacher descendant nearby
narrative_blocks.body: Roger Sherman is buried at Grove Street Cemetery in New Haven — the same cemetery where his great-grandson Sherman Day Thacher (I00592) would be laid to rest 138 years later, in 1931. The continuity of the burial site marks the family's deep New Haven roots across four generations.

burialCemetery.name: Grove Street Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Historic New Haven cemetery. Hezekiah Stanley (d. Sept 11, 1860) and Sally Hooker Stanley (d. Dec 21, 1862) buried here.
